title: Facial Droop - Bell's Palsy
chief_complaint: Sudden onset right facial weakness for 24 hours. Noticed when shaving - right side of face not moving. Unable to close right eye completely.

physical_exam.neuro: Right facial weakness involving forehead and lower face, inability to raise right eyebrow, incomplete eye closure, loss of nasolabial fold, taste decreased on anterior 2/3 of tongue right side, no other neurological deficits

correct_diagnosis: Bell's Palsy (Right Facial Nerve Palsy - Lower Motor Neuron)
key_learning_points: Forehead involvement = LMN (Bell's) vs UMN (stroke spares forehead)

patient_responses.onset: I woke up and couldn't move the right side of my face
patient_responses.pain: Mild pain behind my right ear
patient_responses.other_symptoms: No weakness in arms or legs

feedback.correct_message: Well diagnosed! Bell's palsy with classic LMN pattern.
feedback.incorrect_message: This is Bell's palsy. The forehead involvement confirms LMN lesion.
feedback.attending_note: Bell's palsy: Prednisolone 60mg daily for 5 days started within 72h. Eye protection (lubricating drops, taping at night). Most patients recover fully within 3-6 months.
